w:r.w:t: (Doc 1 of 2 in the Two-Doc Build System)
is the original one I had fro July 2025. Should I combine the format of this older one into this version???
PURPOSE & HOW TO USE THIS DOCUMENT
The MM Build Guide is the comprehensive

(as well as showing the future features to develop in later stages after testing MVP). It provides detailed specifications, technical requirements, dependencies, and design decisions needed to build the Moore Momentum System.
in the Two-Doc Build System. Use it alongside the
- Product specifications, feature requirements, decision authority
Look up full specs, UI requirements, technical considerations
Review dependencies and blockers for prioritization
Check Decision Log for previous decisions or add new ones
Verify if something is MVP vs Post-MVP vs Exploratory
How This Connects To Other Documents
Companion doc - tracks STATUS/WHO while this doc covers WHAT/WHY
Master index - explains how all docs work together
Detailed System Strategy Guide for additional context
A feature spec changes based on validation or technical constraints
A decision is made (add to Decision Log)
Something is learned from testing (add to Learnings Log)
A feature moves between MVP/Post-MVP/Exploratory categories
SECTION 2: PHASE 1 STAGE 1 - Golden Habit Identification
SECTION 3: PHASE 1 STAGE 2 - 3 Momentum Building Methods
FlutterFlow for MVP then migrate to Flutter for Post MVP (UI) + Voiceflow (AI) + Firebase (Backend)
PWA-first via FlutterFlow (no app store approval needed)
🎯 This Week's Priority: Haroon Validation Meeting

The primary goal is to reality-check all 80 MVP features before proceeding with detailed documentation and visual design. This prevents costly rebuilds if technical constraints require feature changes.
Are the 80 features achievable in 4 months with current team size?
Which features should be cut or simplified for MVP?
What's the remaining infrastructure work and time estimate?
Any technical concerns with Voiceflow multi-agent architecture at scale?
How complex is Google Calendar integration (OAuth, two-way sync)?
Are animated sliders with haptic feedback feasible in FlutterFlow?
What's the realistic timeline for Phase 2 gamification features.
Any features that seem technically risky or need prototyping first?
Are we confirmed that we do the MVP in Flutterflow then migrate to Flutter for Post MVP? Full understanding of how this will work and why we’ll need to do this?
Firebase backend connected (Firestore, Auth, Functions, Analytics)
Flutter Flow corresponding screens/animations complete for
Momentum Lists collecting user input (17 lists defined)
Waiting on tagging criteria document from Will
Waiting on Haroon validation before creating wireframes
Need to begin recruiting 50-100 users for Month 3
All 10 features not started: 3 Momentum Building Methods AI logic, IF-THEN planning flow, Habit refinement workflow, Space Cantina unlock.

All 50 features not started: Daily Check-In interface, Hybrid Scoring system, Interactive Animated Sliders, Core Balance Meter, Routines system, Weekly Missions, Gamification (XP, levels, achievements), Calendar integration.
Master Visual Guide (Figma), Default Pain Point examples (final), Screen copy for all features, Animation specifications, Motivational messages library.
SECTION 2: PHASE 1 STAGE 1 - Use Habits Hierarchy to Determine Pain Points and Golden Habits to Resolve Them.
PHASE 1 AND 2 SEQUENCE (SIMPLEST VERSION FOR CODERS)
Overview of phases 1 and 2, goals for both first time and returning players, success metrics, etc at the top.
Example player walkthrough of first time player longer version (including visuals)
PHASE 1 AND PHASE 2 BEST OVERVIEW VERSION

Details of the entire MM System (both phases) and how it should all work together, which the "Phase 1 and 2 Sequence” docs was created from (can reference when get stuck/something not working as originally intended).

Guide players through AI-powered questions to identify personalized Golden Habits via the 5-step Habits Hierarchy Journey. The goal is to move from vague pain point → specific, actionable Golden Habit in
(for initial) and 1-2 minutes for returning, and to introduce the key personalization element of the Command Center/Momentum Lists.
Visual Metaphor: Move through all 5 sections of the
Habits Hierarchy pyramid until complete phase 1 where they see the pyramid was a hybrid for their rocket, which then launches at the end as they complete level 1. et.
Each step builds on the previous, no skipping allowed (creates investment and proper foundation).
- Connect to one of 5 Core Areas
- Match to personalized habit category (leap from generic advice to highly personalized)
- Create specific, actionable Golden Habit (solid actionable/simple starting point that feels achievable).
2.2a Player Pain Points This Phase Solves
Users don't know which habits to start with
AI-guided Golden Habit identification through pain point analysis
Generic habit advice doesn't fit individual
Triple-Check Criteria: WANTS to, CAN do, EFFECTIVE
Users overwhelmed with too many habits
Start with 1-2 Golden Habits per core, expand over time
No self-awareness of strengths/weaknesses, and other personal factors to ensure tailored golden habits
Lack of Trust (players worry about advice being pulled randomly from internet and knowing AI is often wrong)
Command Center containing the Momentum Lists provide a simple database to access all major aspects/personalization factors of a player’s life.
2.2b Known Issues and Challenges to Address

Voiceflow-driven conversations with natural language processing. Multiple specialized AI agents hand off to each other based on conversation context. Adaptive questioning adjusts based on user responses. Context preservation allows users to leave and return without losing progress.
Voiceflow multi-agent setup with defined handoff points
NLP for understanding natural language responses
Knowledge documents uploaded to each agent
Chat interface (ListView for history, TextFormField for input)
Player selects which of the 5 Core Areas to focus on first. Can be AI-recommended based on initial questions or player-chosen. Explains each Core Area briefly before selection.
5 tappable Core Area cards with icons and brief descriptions
Option for AI to recommend based on initial questions

AI identifies specific pain points through open-ended questions. Starts with Core Areas scoring lowest. Uses "Why?" drilling technique to find root cause (not surface symptoms). Validates understanding before proceeding.
"What's holding you back from being your best self?"
"When you say tired, is it physical exhaustion, mental fatigue, or lack of motivation?"
"Got it - so poor sleep quality is affecting your energy. Let me confirm: your main challenge is getting quality sleep, which leaves you physically exhausted. Is that right?"
NLP categorizes pain points to Core Areas (we have core area
Top Pain Points Database for pattern matching
Clarifying questions if input is vague
Collects information for the 17 Momentum Lists during natural conversation flow. This data powers AI personalization throughout the system. Lists are stored in Command Center for player to view/edit.
11. Time Availability | 12. Energy Patterns | 13. Social Support | 14. Resources
15. Past Successes | 16. Past Failures | 17. Back to the Future (vision)
in Phase 2, not just static profile data. Players earn XP for adding/updating entries, creating engagement loop.
Automatically extracts and tags user responses to populate Momentum Lists without explicit data entry. AI identifies when a user mentions a strength, passion, fear, etc. and tags it appropriately.
"I'm really good at organizing and planning, but I freeze when I have to speak in public."
Waiting on Tagging Criteria Document from Will that defines exact rules for what goes in each list.

AI recommends personalized Golden Habits based on pain points, Core Area, and Momentum Lists data. Pulls from Master Habits Database (500+ vetted habits). Explains WHY this habit addresses their specific pain point.
Match pain point → relevant habits in database
Filter by player's strengths (leverage what they're good at)
Filter by player's passions (use what they enjoy)
Filter by time availability (fits their schedule)
Filter by obstacles (avoid known friction points)
Triple-Check Criteria validation ensures every Golden Habit passes three tests before being recommended.
Does player actually want to do this? Feels good, energizing, aligned with passions
Can player realistically do this? Fits schedule, resources, lifestyle constraints
Will this actually address their pain point? Evidence-based, proven approach
After AI recommends habit, player rates each criterion on 1-5 scale. If any score < 3, AI suggests modifications or alternatives. If all pass, proceed to habit creation.
Allows player to customize, adjust, or regenerate Golden Habit recommendation until satisfied. Player should feel ownership over their habit, not like it was imposed.
Adjust frequency, duration, timing, or specific action
Player enters their own habit (validated against criteria)

500+ vetted habits organized by Core Area, pain point, and difficulty level. Each habit includes why it works (behavioral science backing), common pitfalls, and success tips. AI queries database for personalized recommendations.

End-of-flow quality check: "How helpful was this Golden Habit?" (1-5 stars). Collects qualitative feedback: "What would have made this better?" Feeds Data Learning Effects to improve recommendations over time.
This data creates the competitive moat through Data Learning Effects. More users → better recommendations → higher success rates → more users. Critical for long-term differentiation.
Bypass full Habits Hierarchy for experienced users. Natural language shortcut (2-3 min vs 10-15 min). Timeline: 6-12 months post-launch.
AI agent performs setup tasks immediately after Golden Habit identification (calendar, reminders). Timeline: 6-9 months post-launch.

: This is a feature we’re designing for phase 1, stage 2. Once completed, we can bring it into this phase if makes sense to help with the part in the Habits Hierarchy where we help them overcome the biggest obstacle to their golden habit.
🔵 Wearables To Enhance/Reduce Friction To Build Habits:

Use of wearables (i.e. ai glasses, ring, etc) to record day, sync with habits working on, and help player in real time to align their choices/actions with the Golden habits and MBM’s they’ve established.
Voiceflow API integration for AI conversations
Conversation state management (user can leave and return)
Habit database preloaded with 500+ vetted habits
NLP for understanding natural language responses
Estimated completion time: 20-30 minutes per stage
❓ Decision: How many Golden Habits per Core Area in MVP?
Option A: 1 per core (5 total) - simpler
Option B: 2 per core (10 total) - more comprehensive
Option C: User decides (1-3 per core) - flexible
Option C, default to 2 per core
❓ Decision: Can users skip Phase 1 and go straight to Phase 2?
Pro: Some users already know their habits
Con: Missing 3MBMs setup increases failure risk
Allow skip but show warning + offer "Quick Setup" alternative
❓ Decision: Should we show Universal Principles during Phase 1?
Pro: Educate users on why habits work
Con: May slow down flow, information overload
Introduce principles contextually, not as separate lessons
SECTION 3: PHASE 1 STAGE 2 - 3 Momentum Building Methods
Apply the 3 MBMs (Make Obvious, Make Easy, Make Rewarding) to Golden Habits identified in Stage 1. This reduces friction and increases success rate before daily tracking begins.

User leaves Phase 1 with 2-3 Golden Habits per Core Area, fully optimized with 3MBMs, ready for Phase 2 daily tracking. Each habit has been "Momentified" - transformed from generic habit into personalized, friction-reduced, reward-enhanced action.
Habits fail because they're too hard
Make It Easy - break into tiny steps, reduce barriers
Make It Obvious - environmental cues, reminders, triggers
Habits fail because they're not rewarding
Make It Rewarding - immediate rewards, dopamine hits
Explains the science of cues and triggers. Suggests personalized cue strategies based on player's Momentum Lists (schedule, environment, existing routines). Player selects and customizes their cue.
"When I see my gym bag by the door"
Uses player's Time Availability list to suggest realistic timing. Uses Environment list to suggest physical cues. Uses existing routines for action-stacking opportunities.
Explains the science of reducing friction. Suggests ways to shrink habit duration or reduce barriers. Player commits to specific "easy" implementation.
Shrink to starter version ("Read 1 page" not "Read 30 min")
Lay out gym clothes night before
Explains dopamine and immediate rewards. Suggests personalized reward strategies based on player's Passions list. Player designs their reward system.
Proactively identifies obstacles before they happen. Creates IF-THEN plans: "IF [obstacle], THEN [response]." Uses player's Obstacles list from Momentum Lists.
IF I'm too tired to workout, THEN I'll do 5 minutes of stretching instead
IF I'm traveling, THEN I'll use hotel gym or do bodyweight exercises
IF I forget my lunch, THEN I'll choose the healthiest option at nearest restaurant
Combines Golden Habit + 3 MBMs into final "Momentified" habit ready for Phase 2 tracking. Creates summary card showing the complete habit system.
After pouring coffee, before checking phone
Log in app (XP), enjoy coffee mindfully
If running late, do 2-min breathing instead
- Uses all Momentum Lists data for deeply personalized suggestions
- Social hub reward for completing Stage 2
- Don't lose work if user exits mid-flow
- Can leave and return without starting over
- Animation and achievement unlock for finishing Phase 1
Stage 2 must receive all Stage 1 data (Golden Habit details, Momentum Lists)
Each MBM section is a Voiceflow agent with handoffs
Momentified habit stored in Firebase with all components
Progress saved after each MBM completion
Estimated completion time: 15-20 minutes per Golden Habit
Daily habit tracking with gamified rocket ship journey, converting Golden Habits into automatic success habits through consistent action and rewards.
Daily check-in (morning or evening, user's choice)
Make daily check-in feel like a game, not a chore. ALWAYS reward, never punish.
Daily tracking feels tedious and boring
Smart reminders, streak system, countdown timer
Always reward (even low scores earn points), encouraging tone
Too many habits overwhelm daily check-in
Show only active habits, hide completed Success Habits
Hard to maintain balance across all 5 Cores
Core Balance Meter, alerts when core needs attention
Major innovation that maintains the "Firing on All Cylinders" principle while reducing daily friction. Different scoring for Active Cores vs. Maintaining Cores.
(with Golden Habits/Working On habits): Detailed habit-by-habit scoring with specific feedback.
(no active habits yet): Quick 1-5 general score for the whole core. "How's your Physical Health today?" Simple, fast.

Without this, users would have to score 25+ items daily (5 cores × 5 habits each). With Hybrid Scoring, early users might only score 7 items (2 active habits + 5 quick core scores). As they progress and add more habits, scoring naturally expands.

Liquid-filled sliders that respond with haptic feedback. As user slides, liquid fills the container. Score-specific encouragement messages appear. Visual delight makes scoring feel like play, not work.
"Tough day? Tomorrow's a fresh start. You showed up - that counts! +10 XP"
"Not your best, but you're tracking. That's momentum building! +25 XP"
"Great day! You're building real momentum. +75 XP"
"CRUSHING IT! 🔥 This is what growth looks like! +100 XP"
Are animated liquid-fill sliders with haptic feedback feasible in FlutterFlow? What's the complexity level?
- Visual showing balance across all 5 cores with 7-day rolling average
- Consecutive days with visual countdown and Streak Freeze power-up
- Push notifications timed to user schedule
Countdown timers, balance alerts, habit coloring system, skip handling, offline support, accessibility
Buy power-ups (Streak Freeze, Double XP Day)
Daily check-in: 50-100 XP, 15-25 Credits (based on scores)
Level up every ~1,000 XP (scales up at higher levels)
2D rocket (MVP) showing momentum. Journey between planets represents progress. 7-day journeys to planet milestones. Visual upgrades earned through progress.
Simple 2D rocket, basic movement animation, planet progression
3D rocket, detailed planets, obstacles (asteroids), power-ups, alien encounters
- Milestones like "First Golden Habit", "7-Day Streak", "All Cores Balanced"
- Colors, wings, thrusters purchasable with Credits
- Major milestones with alien encounters teaching Universal Principles
- Momentum Lists updates as quests for bonus XP
- Asteroids and aliens that appear when progress stalls
- Streak Freeze, Double XP Day, Momentum Boost
Leaderboards, tribes, celebration animations, sound effects, confetti, haptic feedback
Firestore database, Authentication (Email, Google, Apple Sign-In), Cloud Functions, Hosting, Storage, Analytics all configured and working.
Central hub for viewing/editing all 17 Momentum Lists. Hamburger menu navigation. Real-time Firebase sync. Currently in development.
Two-way sync (MM → Calendar, Calendar → MM). AI agent creates habit reminders. OAuth flow implementation required.
How complex is the OAuth implementation for Google Calendar? Time estimate?
Separate analytics collection for aggregate queries
Built for Data Learning Effects from Day 1
PWA via FlutterFlow - Fast to deploy, works on all devices, no app store approval, easy updates
Continue PWA, monitor performance - Collect data, identify any limitations
Evaluate native migration - If need advanced features (3D, AR), export Flutter code from FlutterFlow
Purpose: Track key decisions and their rationale to avoid re-debating and maintain consistency.
Better quality, more validation iterations, sustainable pace. Month 4 enables extended beta testing (50+ users) and professional launch process.
Slightly longer to market, extra month of costs.
Validate AI logic in Chipp.ai before production implementation.
1-2 weeks validating is cheaper than 4 weeks building wrong thing. Real user feedback before code. Dual-track approach keeps Haroon productive during validation.
Launch as PWA via FlutterFlow, not native apps.
Faster deployment, no app store approval, works on all devices, easy updates. Can migrate to native later if needed for 3D/AR features.
Use MM Build Guide (this doc) + MM Build Tracker (Google Sheet) instead of multiple overlapping documents.
Reduces friction, clear separation of WHAT/WHY (Guide) vs STATUS/WHO (Tracker). Consolidates Issues & Features doc and 3 Month Master Plan into actionable format.
Pending 1: Golden Habits per Core Area
A) 1 per core (5 total), B) 2 per core (10 total), C) User decides (1-3)
Option C, default to 2 per core.
Can users skip Phase 1 and go straight to Phase 2?
Allow skip with warning + offer "Quick Setup" alternative.
A) Streak breaks, B) 1 missed day allowed/month, C) Streak Freeze power-up
Are liquid-fill animated sliders with haptic feedback feasible in FlutterFlow?
What's realistic for MVP - full two-way sync or just push to calendar?
Purpose: Document insights from testing, development, and user feedback to improve future iterations.
Comprehensive docs essential for fundraising but need condensed versions for daily execution
Progressive disclosure (Quick Start → Reference → Deep Dive) serves both immediate team needs and future scaling
Two-Doc System (Build Guide + Build Tracker) reduces friction vs. multiple overlapping documents
Validate features with developer BEFORE building detailed documentation to avoid rework
AI-First validation (Chipp.ai testing) is faster and cheaper than building first
Dual-track approach (Will validates while Haroon builds infrastructure) maintains momentum
Better to spend 1-2 weeks validating than 4 weeks building wrong thing
Test with real users in lightweight environment before production code
Daily interactions should feel rewarding, not burdensome
Animated sliders with haptic feedback increase engagement
Score-specific encouragement messages create emotional connection
ALWAYS reward (even low scores) - never punish users for showing up
Hybrid Scoring System critical for reducing friction while maintaining "Firing on All Cylinders"
FlutterFlow enables rapid development with path to native if needed
Voiceflow multi-agent architecture handles complex AI conversations well
Firebase provides excellent real-time sync for habit tracking
PWA deployment much faster than app store process
[To be filled in after validation meeting]
